notes: VAHAN is a dynamic JSF application requiring manual browser interaction. Data below compiled from VAHAN dashboard screenshots, CEIC Data, Mahindra Annual Report FY2024, Mobility Foresights India Truck Market 2024, and CRISIL Ratings.

india_commercial_vehicle_fleet_compositi.description: Breakdown of Indian commercial vehicle (goods carrier) fleet by category
sources: CRISIL Ratings FY27 forecast (Economic Times, Apr 2025)

lcv_goods_below_3_5t.note: LCV Goods <3.5T dominate CV sales at 47.6% (Mahindra AR FY24). LCVs account for 65-70% of total truck sales annually (Mobility Foresights).

mhcv_goods_above_3_5t.note: M&HCV trucks (>3.5T) account for ~30-35% of truck sales. Tata Motors leads with ~55% market share in M&HCV segment.

total_cv_volumes_fy26.note: ~11 lakh units in FY26 with 13% YoY growth. Expected to reach 12.4 lakh in FY27 (CRISIL).

monthly_transport_registrations_dec2025.note: VAHAN monthly transport vehicle registrations (CEIC Data, Dec 2025)

fleet_ratio_for_freight.description: HCV vs LCV ratio specifically for FREIGHT (goods movement)
fleet_ratio_for_freight.note: By NUMBER of vehicles, LCVs dominate (65%). By TONNAGE carried, HCVs dominate (70%) because each HCV carries 3-10x more than an LCV. For our optimization (which is tonnage-based), the relevant ratio is HCV:LCV = 70:30 by freight volume.
sources: NITI Aayog & RMI (2021). Fast Tracking Freight in India.

key_findings_for_model.1_fleet_ratio: Use HCV:LCV = 70:30 by tonnage (already set in config.py as hcv_lcv_fleet_ratio=0.70)
key_findings_for_model.2_truck_speed: Average loaded speed 30-35 km/h on highways (already corrected to 35 km/h in config.py)
key_findings_for_model.3_hcv_capacity: Typical HCV payload 10,000 kg (matches config.py hcv_capacity=10000)
key_findings_for_model.4_lcv_capacity: Typical LCV payload 3,000 kg (matches config.py lcv_capacity=3000)
